subject: [PATCH 06/13] mm: add PMD swap entry splitting support

2026-04-27.patch_summary: This patch adds support for splitting a PMD swap entry into 512 PTE swap entries in the Linux kernel's memory management (mm) module. This is done by adding a new branch to the __split_huge_pmd_locked() function that handles PMD swap entries differently than migration splits, as swap entries point to swap slots rather than pages. The patch also updates the pmd_is_valid_softleaf() function to recognize swap entries as valid leaf entries. The changes are made to support partial-range mprotect and munmap operations, as well as PMD-COW fallbacks and swap-in/swap-off fallbacks. The patch aims to improve memory management efficiency by allowing for more fine-grained control over swap entry splitting.
